acceptance.proof: A finite bit-preserving ClockOffset value rejects NaN and infinities with unchanged bits.
One explicit call adds an accepted offset to an accepted RawSourceTimestamp and returns the existing ClockCorrected DerivedTimestamp while retaining both inputs separately.
Finite extreme inputs whose sum is non-finite return a stable typed error with unchanged result bits and no derived timestamp.

No offset selection, history, automatic mapping, clock/timer read, packet, I/O, UDP, retries, scheduling, smoothing, dejitter, currentness, runtime activation, dependency, device, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold authority is added.

commit_policy: Commit the explicit offset application separately from lifecycle acceptance and push preparation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route explicit offset application and automatic/runtime exclusions.
Synchronize public candidate and non-scope prose.
Review public/private routing; no shared-skill mutation expected.
Review explicit mapping and authority separation; no shared-skill mutation expected.

non_scope: Offset acquisition/selection/history, automatic mapping or post-processing, clock/timer reads, packet shapes, I/O, sockets, UDP, retries, scheduling, or provider evidence.
Smoothing, dejitter, drift fitting, currentness, accuracy, reachability, interoperability, or runtime activation.
Dependencies, features, unsafe, FFI, devices, private artifacts, black-box observation, or Manifold authority.
objective: Add the smallest explicit dependency-free finite clock-offset value and fallible caller-invoked addition to an accepted raw timestamp, returning the existing ClockCorrected derived-timestamp type without selecting offsets, retaining history, reading clocks, or activating post-processing.
outputs: Explicit application result retaining raw timestamp and offset beside derived timestamp, plus damaged overflow tests.
Public routing/validation documentation, Standard receipt, and exact-ID executed no-force feature-push receipt.
